summary: Clear soundboard recording captures a standout 8‑25‑72 show with a monster Other One → Comes A Time jam and superb Jack Straw, despite being only a partial set.
review: The audience‑sourced soundboard tape delivers excellent separation and balance, letting every nuance of Jerry’s white Strat shine.  The performance is praised as one of the best of ’72 – tight first‑set numbers like Black‑Throated Wind, Loser and El Paso flow into a second‑set monster featuring Other One → Comes A Time and an exquisite Jack Straw.  The only drawback noted is that the recording appears to be incomplete, covering just part of the concert.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers give 4‑5 stars and highlight exceptional playing and a very clear recording, though several note the set is only partial which keeps the rating from being higher.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear, well‑balanced mix with perfect instrument separation; some reviewers call it "best concert sound" while others note only a partial (first‑set) capture.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Early on‑stage tech hiccups gave way to tight ensemble playing; the first set is strong and the second set delivers expansive jams, though the recording’s incompleteness affects overall flow.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for a "phat white Strat", perfect tone, poignant playing on Comes A Time and immaculate dual‑guitar work.
